title: Attribute
description: Structured product attribute for measurable facts (battery life, display size, weight, storage). Same shape as schema.org PropertyValue. Use tags for unstructured categorical labels, options for variant-defining axes, and metadata for business-specific data.

name.description: Human-readable attribute name (e.g., 'Battery life', 'Display', 'Weight'). Same role as schema.org PropertyValue.name. Clients SHOULD compare names case-insensitively when correlating attributes across merchants.

key.description: Optional stable identifier paired with `name` for cross-merchant correlation and variant override matching. Same role as schema.org PropertyValue.propertyID. Open vocabulary; clients MUST tolerate unknown values. Vertical extensions MAY standardize keys for specific categories.

value.description: Display value. Same role as schema.org PropertyValue.value. Can be a number, range, or domain notation.

numeric_value.description: Optional comparable numeric form of `value`. Businesses SHOULD populate this when the attribute is a single measurement so agents can filter without parsing the display string.

unit.description: Optional unit of measurement. Open vocabulary; clients MUST tolerate unknown values. Producers SHOULD prefer SI/UCUM-style symbols (`kg`, `mm`, `Hz`) where they exist; common catalog units (`GB`, `inches`, `hours`) are well-known alternatives.
